sourceMovie.title: Cult of Chucky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Cult of Chucky (2017) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Don Mancini's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cult of Chucky clicked for you, Halloween (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cult of Chucky?
faq.answer: Try Halloween, Halloween, Terrifier 3—each shares DNA with Cult of Chucky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cult of Chucky?
faq.answer: Cult of Chucky (2017) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cult of Chucky part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Child's Play Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
